macro_markets.query: What are the most important macroeconomic and stock market news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major index movements, Fed/central bank news, and significant company earnings or announcements.

items.title: Global Chip and AI Selloff Triggers Market Drop After China's Moonshot AI Unveils Kimi K3
items.summary: A global selloff in chip and AI stocks occurred Friday after China's Moonshot AI unveiled Kimi K3, a 2–3 trillion parameter open-weight model, causing Japan's SoftBank to fall 9%, Tokyo Electron down over 8%, and TSMC to drop 7.29%.

items.title: Apple Briefly Overtakes Nvidia as World's Most Valuable Company Amid Tech Volatility
items.summary: Apple regained the top spot with a $4.88 trillion valuation, briefly overtaking Nvidia which dropped 3.5%, before Nvidia recovered to close slightly ahead as the AI-driven rally faced sustainability concerns.

items.title: U.S. Stock Futures Slide and Oil Soars as Trump Declares Iran Ceasefire 'Over'
items.summary: Stock markets dropped worldwide and Brent crude nears $85 a barrel after President Donald Trump raised doubts about the temporary truce with Iran, intensifying fears of renewed Middle East conflict and higher gasoline prices.

items.title: Fed Policymakers Swell for Rate Hikes as Cleveland Fed President Hammack Calls for Action on Inflation
items.summary: Cleveland Fed President Beth Hammack joined a growing chorus of policymakers arguing interest rates may need to rise to combat persistent inflation, setting up a charged debate at the Fed's July meeting under Chairman Kevin Warsh.

items.title: U.S. Inflation Cools Sharply in June with CPI Falling 0.4% as Energy Prices Drop 5.7%
items.summary: Headline U.S. CPI fell 0.4% month-over-month in June, the largest decline since April 2020, driven by a 9.7% drop in gasoline prices, though core inflation remained elevated at 2.6% year-over-year.

items.title: China's Q2 GDP Growth Slows to 4.3% Below Government Target as Economic Unevenness Persists
items.summary: China's economic growth slowed to 4.3% year-over-year in Q2 from 5.0% in Q1, falling below the government's 4.5%–5.0% target range, highlighting uneven growth despite strong export earnings from EV production.

items.title: U.S. Imposes 25% Tariffs on Some Brazilian Imports Over Unfair Trade Practices
items.summary: The U.S. announced new 25% tariffs on certain Brazilian imports including after a yearlong investigation concluded Brazil engaged in unfair trade practices, though beef, coffee, and rare-earth materials escaped the tariffs.

items.title: Big Tech Earnings Week Begins: Alphabet, Tesla, and Intel Set to Test AI Trade After Chip Selloff
items.summary: Alphabet reports as the first hyperscaler next week, followed by Tesla and Intel, serving as a key test for the AI trade after the recent chip selloff and concerns over high profit expectations.

items.title: Crypto Market Tumbles as Chip Selloff Spills Into Bitcoin and Ether
items.summary: Bitcoin dipped below $63,000 and Ether fell 1.74% as the chip selloff spilled into crypto Friday, with the total crypto market cap shedding 1.86% to $2.16 trillion.

items.title: U.S. Regional Banks Deliver Broad Q2 Gains Alleviating Middle East War Concerns
items.summary: U.S. Bancorp and PNC Financial delivered broad second-quarter gains relying on a lending rebound and strong fee income, alleviating concerns that the Middle East war would weigh on loan demand.

crypto_news.query: What are the most significant cryptocurrency and blockchain news from the past 24 hours? Include major price movements, regulatory news, and notable project developments.

items.title: Bitcoin Slips Below $62K Amid US–Iran Conflict as $350M Liquidated
items.summary: Bitcoin fell more than 2% to around $62,000 following Iranian ballistic missile and drone strikes on a US base in Jordan, triggering a sharp risk-off move and $350 million in leveraged liquidations across crypto markets[8].

items.title: US Regulators Miss GENIUS Act Deadline for Final Stablecoin Rules
items.summary: US regulators failed to meet the GENIUS Act's one-year deadline for issuing final stablecoin regulations, a key milestone passed since the act was signed by President Trump on July 19, 2025[1].

items.title: Uniswap Governance Votes on v4 Fees and Robinhood Chain Expansion
items.summary: Uniswap governance is voting on Sunday to enable protocol fees for v4 pools on seven chains and fees for v2/v3 on Robinhood Chain, a move poised to increase UNI token burns[1][7].

items.title: Robinhood Chain DEX Volume Surges Past $528M, Ranking Fourth Across Chains
items.summary: Robinhood Chain's DEX trading volume exceeded $528.5 million in the past 24 hours, securing the fourth-highest ranking across all chains amid growing native protocol interest[3].

items.title: FTX to Distribute $900 Million to Creditors in Fifth Payout Wave
items.summary: FTX announced it will distribute roughly $900 million to creditors in its fifth wave of payouts, continuing the long-awaited restitution process for the collapsed exchange's victims[1].

items.title: Coinbase CEO Armstrong Predicts Bitcoin Bottom Near $60K
items.summary: Coinbase CEO Brian Armstrong stated Bitcoin likely bottomed around $60,000, citing the four-year halving cycle and advising traders to watch the realized price at $53,600[8].

items.title: TOSHI Token Rallies 17% to $54M Market Cap on July 18
items.summary: The TOSHI token surged 17.07% on July 18, reaching a spot price of $0.00012861 and a market cap exceeding $54 million amid broader market volatility[5].

items.title: South Korean Regulator Initiates Sanctions Against Upbit Operator Dunamu
items.summary: The South Korean financial regulator has begun sanction procedures against Upbit operator Dunamu, while the Financial Commission plans to introduce an AI monitoring system for virtual asset regulation[3].

items.title: France Orders ISPs to Block Polymarket Over Addictive Mechanics
items.summary: France's regulator ordered internet service providers to block Polymarket, citing concerns over addictive mechanics, lack of self-exclusion tools, and users bypassing financial restrictions[7].

items.title: Michael Saylor Opposes BIP 110 Proposal, Warns of Threat to Bitcoin Neutrality
items.summary: MicroStrategy founder Michael Saylor publicly opposed the BIP 110 proposal, arguing it threatens Bitcoin's neutrality and open market principles by targeting specific uses through consensus rules[6][7].

ai_news.query: What are the most important artificial intelligence and machine learning news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include new model releases, company announcements, research breakthroughs, and industry trends.

items.title: Chinese Startup Moonshot AI Unveils Kimi K3, World's Largest Open-Weight AI Model
items.summary: Moonshot AI released Kimi K3, a 2.8-trillion-parameter model with a 1-million-token context window, claiming performance rivaling Anthropic's Fable and approaching OpenAI's capabilities; it hit #1 on the AI coding Arena benchmark within hours.

items.title: Meta Introduces Muse Image for AI Image Generation and Editing
items.summary: Meta launched Muse Image, a new artificial intelligence model enabling users to create and edit images with advanced generative capabilities.

items.title: Anthropic Deploys Claude Sonnet 5 and Restores Fable and Mythos Models
items.summary: Anthropic launched Claude Sonnet 5 and restored access to its frontier Fable and Mythos models following a federal export control review, with Fable 5 now included in top-tier subscriptions.

items.title: NVIDIA Releases Nemotron 3 Embed as Top Retrieval Benchmark Model
items.summary: NVIDIA released Nemotron 3 Embed as three open checkpoints under the OpenMDW-1.1 license, with its 8B BF16 model scoring #1 overall on retrieval benchmarks (78.5% RTEB, 75.5% MMTEB).

items.title: OpenAI Unveils GPT-Red to Cut Prompt-Injection Attacks on GPT-5.6
items.summary: OpenAI introduced GPT-Red, an automated red-teaming LLM that reduced prompt-injection attack success on GPT-5.6 from 90% to 23%, outperforming human red-teamers by 84% vs 13% on a 2025 benchmark.

items.title: IBM Research Unveils Breakthrough Analog AI Chip for Efficient Deep Learning
items.summary: IBM Research announced a groundbreaking analog AI chip demonstrating remarkable efficiency and accuracy in performing complex computations for deep neural networks.

items.title: Apple Overtook Nvidia to Become World's Most Valuable Company Amid AI Reassessment
items.summary: Apple surpassed Nvidia on Friday to become the world's most valuable company as investors reassess AI outlooks, reshuffling tech heavyweight rankings.

items.title: Nationwide Protests Planned Against AI Data Center Expansion Across 125 U.S. Locations
items.summary: Opponents of rapid AI data center buildout, coordinated by HumansFirst, plan Saturday protests in at least 125 U.S. locations, marking the first coordinated national effort against AI infrastructure expansion.

items.title: SpaceX in Talks to Provide U.S. Defense with Billion-Dollar Data Center Capacity for AI
items.summary: Elon Musk's SpaceX is negotiating to provide the U.S. Department of Defense with data center capacity worth billions of dollars to run AI models, according to Wall Street Journal reports.

items.title: Coatue Management Leads $3 Billion Investment in Databricks at $188 Billion Valuation
items.summary: Investment firm Coatue Management is leading a $3 billion investment in data analytics provider Databricks, valuing the company at $188 billion, per Wall Street Journal reports.

items.title: Japan Plans to Buy Thousands of Nvidia Chips to Power National AI Ecosystem
items.summary: The Japanese government plans to purchase thousands of Nvidia's next-generation semiconductors to build an AI ecosystem on its own soil, supporting its artificial-intelligence push.

items.title: Apple Files Lawsuit Accusing OpenAI of Stealing Trade Secrets for ChatGPT Hardware
items.summary: Apple accused OpenAI on Friday of stealing trade secrets as the company seeks to build its own hardware for ChatGPT, escalating tensions between the tech giants.

items.title: Major League Baseball Restricts Dugout iPad Use to Prevent AI-Influenced Decisions
items.summary: MLB is restricting iPad usage in dugouts to prevent artificial intelligence from influencing game decisions, addressing growing concerns about AI in sports.

items.title: EU Issues New Rules Requiring Google to Share Search Data and Open Android to Rival AI Companies
items.summary: The European Union issued new regulations requiring Google to share search data and open its Android system to rival AI companies, intensifying regulatory pressure on the tech giant.

items.title: Indonesia Prepares Copyright Law Changes Granting AI Content Privileges to Users
items.summary: Indonesia is drafting sweeping copyright law changes that would grant copyright privileges to people using AI to generate content, potentially triggering a showdown with major tech platforms.

gaming.query: What are the most notable video game and gaming industry news from the past 24 hours? Include game releases, announcements, esports, and industry developments.

items.title: Bethesda Confirms New Fallout Game Developed by Obsidian with Josh Sawyer
items.summary: Bethesda officially confirmed that Obsidian Entertainment is developing a new Fallout game, seemingly independent of Fallout 5, with Josh Sawyer returning as director after nearly four years on the Marathon project.

items.title: Mistfall Hunter Launches July 29 on PC, PS5, and Xbox
items.summary: Skystone Games and Bellring Games announced that the fantasy PvPvE action game Mistfall Hunter will launch on July 29, 2026, with Steam pages already live for the cross-platform release.

items.title: Assassin's Creed Black Flag Resynced Hits 3 Million Sales in One Week
items.summary: The remastered naval combat game surpassed 3 million copies sold within a week of launch and will receive a New Game+ mode, confirming strong commercial performance.

items.title: Nintendo Ends Mobile Support for Mario Kart Tour in September
items.summary: Nintendo is retreating further from mobile gaming by ending support for Mario Kart Tour in September, though it continues to release select mobile exceptions.

items.title: Oblivion Remastered Confirmed for Nintendo Switch 2 on August 11
items.summary: The remastered version of The Elder Scrolls IV: Oblivion will launch exclusively on Nintendo Switch 2 on August 11, 2026, as part of the console's early lineup.

items.title: EA Acquisition by Saudi Fund and Silver Lake for $55B Awaiting EU Approval
items.summary: The European Commission is poised to approve the $55 billion acquisition of Electronic Arts by Saudi Arabia's Public Investment Fund, Affinity Partners, and Silver Lake, which would transfer ownership of Battlefield, The Sims, and FC franchises.

items.title: Xbox May Stop Adding New Day-One Game Pass Releases Amid Internal Backlash
items.summary: Bloomberg's Jason Schreier reports strong internal backlash against Game Pass, suggesting Xbox could rethink its strategy and potentially stop launching new games on the service day-one.

items.title: The Blood of Dawnwalker Will Ship Full Game on Physical Disc
items.summary: Bandai Namco confirmed that the physical boxed version of The Blood of Dawnwalker will include the complete game on Blu-ray, eliminating the need for download codes, with a September 3, 2026 release date.

items.title: SEGA Reveals Gamescom 2026 Lineup Including First Play of Alien: Isolation 2
items.summary: SEGA confirmed its Gamescom 2026 showcase will feature playable demos of Alien: Isolation 2, Crazy Taxi: World Tour, Persona 4 Revival, Stranger Than Heaven, and Total War: Warhammer 40,000.

items.title: Nioh 3 Demo Now Available on PS5 with 2026 Release Confirmed
items.summary: Team Ninja announced that Nioh 3 is coming in 2026, and players can currently try its demo on PlayStation 5 ahead of the full release.

tech_news.query: What are the most important technology news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major product launches, company news, and significant tech industry developments. Exclude AI-specific news.

items.title: Apple Reclaims Title as World's Most Valuable Company, Overtaking Nvidia
items.summary: Apple surpassed Nvidia on Friday to become the world's most valuable company, reshuffling tech heavyweight rankings as investors reassess the AI-driven surge's sustainability.

items.title: China Completes World's First Commercial Brain-Chip Implant
items.summary: China has achieved a neurotechnology milestone by completing the world's first commercial implant of a coin-sized brain-computer interface, marking a breakthrough in its race against Elon Musk's Neuralink.

items.title: Shanghai Xingshu Tiansuan Launches First Space-Computing Satellite Constellation
items.summary: Shanghai Xingshu Tiansuan Space Technology Co. launched the first constellation of a space-computing project on Saturday, with a total goal of deploying 1,000 satellites.

items.title: Samsung Galaxy Watch 9 Confirmed to Use Qualcomm Snapdragon Wear Elite Chip
items.summary: Leaked images confirm the upcoming Galaxy Watch 9 will feature Qualcomm's Snapdragon Wear Elite, signaling Samsung's shift away from its long-standing use of Exynos chipsets.

items.title: Major League Baseball Restricts Dugout iPad Use to Prevent AI Decision-Making
items.summary: MLB has officially restricted iPad usage in dugouts to prevent artificial intelligence from influencing game decisions, a move to maintain human judgment in the sport.

items.title: Waymo Temporarily Halts San Francisco Robotaxi Operations Due to Power Outage
items.summary: Waymo paused its robotaxi services in San Francisco following a PG&E power outage affecting 7,000 customers, citing a need to monitor local conditions before resuming.

items.title: EU Forces Google to Open Android to Rival AI Chatbots and Share Search Data
items.summary: The European Union issued new rules requiring Google to open its Android system to rival AI companies and reveal internal search data, though Google argues this harms user privacy.

items.title: New York Blocks New Large AI Data Centers for Up to One Year
items.summary: New York state will block the construction of new large data centers that fuel AI infrastructure for up to a year to protect the environment and the energy grid.

items.title: Meta Wins Court Bid to Lay Off Employees in AI Discrimination Case
items.summary: A U.S. judge rejected a bid by 26 Meta employees to block layoffs while they pursue claims that AI-powered tools targeted them for job cuts due to disabilities or medical leave.

items.title: Changzhou Announces Construction of China's First City-Level Green Token Factory
items.summary: Changzhou is building China's first city-level green token factory, expected to produce 60 trillion tokens annually to support the country's AI clean-power ambitions.
